evidence: Task fully specified by DEFERRED.md #003: save_state non-atomic write → crash mid-write corrupts .workflow-state.json. Fix scope: workflow-gate.py save_state() only. No open questions.

Atomic-write idiom: serialize to STATE_FILE.with_name(name+'.tmp') in same directory, then tmp.replace(STATE_FILE). Path.replace() = atomic rename on same filesystem (os.replace/MoveFileEx); handles dest-exists on Windows. Crash before replace → STATE_FILE untouched (old state preserved); leftover .tmp harmless (next write overwrites).

V1 5 repeated writes → valid JSON + dest-exists replace OK; V2 no .tmp leftover; V3 tmp same-dir as STATE_FILE (atomic-rename precondition); V4 non-serializable state → json.dumps raises → STATE_FILE preserved unchanged (crash-safety property confirmed)

note: Fixed .tmp name races concurrent invocations; cwd-relative tmp breaks same-dir guarantee if cwd changes; Windows PermissionError on locked dest + leftover .tmp never cleaned.

evidence: Re-VERIFY after Adversary r1 fixes: V1 valid JSON write; V2 tmp has PID infix (.workflow-state.json.<pid>.tmp); V3 cmd_reset swept 2 stale tmps; V4 failed write → STATE preserved + finally-unlink leaves no leftover tmp. All 3 findings addressed.

Adversary cold-start r1: APPROVED_WITH_WARNINGS, 3 WARN 0 BLOCK.
